extrastuffsplashtext_95:
In the beginning Cranky created the songs and the game. Now the game was formless and empty, songs were over the surface of the deep, and the Spirit of Cranky was hovering over the waters.

And Cranky said, "Let there be squares," and there were squares. Cranky saw that squares were good, and he separated the squares from the songs. Cranky called the songs "beats," and the squares he called "blocks." And there were holds, and there were mines-the first chart.

And Cranky said, "Let there be a note between the objects to separate mines from holds." So Cranky made the note and separated the holds under the vault from the mines above it. And it was so. Cranky called the note "mineholds." And there were mines, and there were holds-the second chart.

And Cranky said, "Let the blocks under the sky be gathered to one place, separated by angle and time, and let diamonds appear." And it was so. Cranky called the diamonds "inverses," and the gathered blocks he called "bounces." And Cranky saw that it was good.

Then Cranky said, "Let the blocks produce clicks: accuracy-bearing notes and frontly untouchtable notes on the land that bear accuracy with misses in it, according to their various kinds." And it was so. The blocks produced clicks: notes bearing accuracy according to their kinds and blocks bearing clicks in it according to their kinds. And Cranky saw that it was good. Cranky called the clicks "taps," and the frontly untouchable notes he called "sides." And there were mines, and there were holds-the third chart.

And Cranky said, "Let there be gaps in the beats of the songs to separate the synced from the unsynced, and let them serve as signs to mark sacred times, and divisions and rhythms, and let there be atoms in the notes of the sky to give rhythm to Beatblock." And it was so. Cranky made two great lights-the greater upbeat to govern the divisions and the lesser downbeats to govern the rhythms. He also made the rhythms. Cranky set them in the notes of the sky to give rhythm to Beatblock, to govern the divisions and the rhythms, and to separate the synced from the unsynced. And cranky set atoms in the sky to mark sacred charts, the first synced beats. And Cranky saw that it was good. And there were holds, and there were mines-the fourth chart.

And Cranky said, "Let Beatblock teem with living charters, and let customs fly above Beatblock across the atoms of the sky." So Cranky created the great charters of the Beatblock and every Custom Level with which the rhythm teems and that moves about in it, according to their kinds, and every Charter according to its kind. And Cranky saw that it was good. Cranky blessed them and said, "Be fruitful and increase in number and fill the lands with charts, and let the charts increase on the earth." And there were mines, and there were holds-the fourth chart.

And Cranky said, "Let Beatblock produce charts according to their kinds: the regulars, the ones conforming to the vast, and the gimmicky, each according to its kind." And it was so. Cranky made the charts according to their kinds, the gimmicky according to their kinds, and all the creatures that followed the herd or stood out unique. And Cranky saw that it was good.

Then Cranky said, "Let us make charters in our image, in our likeness, so that they may rule over the mines in the sea and the blocks in the sky, over the holds and all the mineholds, and over all the charts teeming over the surface of Beatblock."

So Cranky created mankind in his own image,
in the image of Cranky he created them;
regular and jumpbeaters he created them.

Cranky blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and increase in number; fill Beatblock and subdue it. Rule over the mines in the sea and the blocks in the sky and over all the charts teeming over the surface of Beatblock."

Then Cranky said, "I give you every note-bearing chart on the face of the whole earth and every chart that has blocks with taps in it. They will be yours to play. And to all the charts of the Beatblock and the blocks in the sky and all the charts teeming along the fringes of beatblock-everything that has the breath of gameplay in it-I give every good chart for playing." And it was so.

Cranky saw all that he had made, and it was very good. And there was evening, and there was morning-the sixth chart.
